meaning: n.<英>变音,转调,弯曲,[语]曲折变化,[数]拐点
examples: Inflexion is a characteristic for the verbs of English.
At the conclusion, the inflexion of the narrative perspective was supplemented.
The point of minima value and the point of inflexion are two common concepts of derivative.
"This is a case of the governor getting his way by the inflexion of his eyebrows, " said a source.
The rule of thumb is that a 10pc rise in crude cuts US growth by 0.2pc four quarters later, but the science is flabbily soft and nobody knows where the inflexion point lies.
